channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Chess grandmaster Magnus Carlsen returns to a tournament after a dispute over jeans is resolved
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Top-ranked chess player Magnus Carlsen is headed back to the World Blitz Championship on Monday after its governing body agreed to loosen a dress code that got him fined and denied a late-round game in another tournament for refusing to change out of jeans.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Notable quotes by Jimmy Carter
item.description: Some quotations from Jimmy Carter. We have a tendency to exalt ourselves and to dwell on the weaknesses and mistakes of others. I have come to realize that in every person there is something fine and pure and noble, along with a desire for self-fulfillment.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Photos: Jimmy Carter, from the White House to building houses
item.description: From The White House to building houses, here's a look at former president Jimmy Carter's life in photos.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Significant milestones in life and career of Jimmy Carter
item.description: — Oct. 1, 1924: James Earl Carter Jr. is born in Plains, Georgia, son of James Sr. and Lillian Gordy Carter. — June 1946: Carter graduates from the U.S. Naval Academy. — July 1946: Carter marries Rosalynn Smith, in Plains.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Five defining moments of Jimmy Carter's presidency
item.description: Former President Carter's post-presidential life lasted decades, putting his career in the White House in a deep rearview mirror in many ways. Carter died Sunday, according to the Carter Center.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Neukam, WGN 9

item.title: In Photos: Jimmy Carter
item.description: Former President Jimmy Carter died on Sunday, according to the Carter Center. Here's a look at his life and times in photos. He had turned 100 in October, making him the longest-lived president in the nation’s history.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, NewsNation

item.title: US children are struggling with reading
item.description: Learning to read can become exponentially more difficult for older students and adults who have fallen behind, creating grave concerns as the number of individuals struggling with literacy into high school and adulthood is increasing.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: These glasses let you 'see' conversations in real time
item.description: When I heard about AI glasses that let you "see" what people are saying, I was skeptical. But after trying them on, I was proven wrong.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Oregon men search for Sasquatch, die in Washington forest
item.description: Two men from Portland searching for Sasquatch and missing since early Christmas morning were found dead in the Gifford Pinchot National Forest, likely from exposure and being ill-prepared.
item.dc:creator: Tim Steele, KRON4

item.title: Nasty norovirus is back in full force with US cases of the stomach virus surging
item.description: Cases of a wretched stomach bug are surging in parts of the United States this winter, according to government data.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Texas mayor confirms fatality from Brazoria County tornado
item.description: "Please join me in praying for all those impacted," Mayor Davis shared in a statement on social media, expressing condolences and support for the affected community.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Estrada, NewsNation

item.title: Kids learning magic of science with dancing candy canes
item.description: SCRANTON, LACKAWANNA COUNTY (WBRE/WYOU) — Local kids are learning the magic of science while having some festive fun on Saturday. Roughly 30 kids filled the Lackawanna County Children's Library on Saturday. They gathered for their Curiosity Club's Candy Cane Science Event.
item.dc:creator: Julia Priest, NewsNation

item.title: VIDEO: Driver fleeing at 120 mph slams into trooper in Florida
item.description: A driver is facing several charges after fleeing Florida Highway Patrol troopers, and slamming into their patrol vehicle at speeds over 120 mph in Tampa
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WGN 9

item.title: Videos show apparent tornadoes moving through Mississippi
item.description: FRANKLIN COUNTY, Miss. (WJTV) - An apparent tornado moved through Franklin County on Saturday, December 28. Karla Wallace, a WJTV 12 News viewer, submitted a video that showed the storm moving through the McCall Creek area.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlin Howell, NewsNation

item.title: LDS mission president from Utah dies 'unexpectedly' in France
item.description: A mission president for The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints died on Saturday, Dec. 28 in France, according to a spokesperson for the church.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: After predicting the financial growth of 2024, here's what 2 economists see in 2025
item.description: Heading into 2024, we said the U.S. economy would likely continue growing, in spite of pundits’ forecast that a recession would strike.
item.dc:creator: Fox 5

item.title: Child critically wounded after accidentally shooting herself in the thigh
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. -- A child is fighting for her life after accidentally shooting herself on Friday, Memphis Police said. At 3:03 p.m., officers responded to an accidental injury in the 2500 block of Elmscourt Drive.
item.dc:creator: Raven Moore, NewsNation

item.title: Sexual misconduct charge against Vancouver teacher dropped due to statute of limitations
item.description: A charge against a Vancouver English teacher for alleged sexual misconduct with a student was dropped Thursday, due to the expiration of the statute of limitations, court documents say.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Deml, NewsNation

item.title: At least 15 people injured after Florida train hits fire truck
item.description: Three firefighters and 12 Brightline passengers were injured Saturday morning following a collision between a Delray Beach Fire Rescue truck and a train.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, ABC 4

item.title: Trailblazing model Dayle Haddon dies from suspected carbon monoxide poisoning
item.description: Dayle Haddon, an actor, activist and trailblazing former “Sports Illustrated” model who pushed back against age discrimination by reentering the industry as a widow, has died in a Pennsylvania home from what authorities believe was carbon monoxide poisoning.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: LIST: Top 10 wackiest Florida Man (and Woman) headlines of 2024
item.description: Another year has come and gone, but the wacky antics of Florida residents will live on forever through the internet.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, ABC 4

item.title: Big Lots reaches deal to keep hundreds of US stores open
item.description: The discount chain Big Lots, which filed for bankruptcy protection in September, has reached a deal that will keep hundreds of its stores and distribution centers open.
item.dc:creator: Dee-Ann Durbin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Las Vegas woman killed in Tyson Foods plant explosion in Georgia, others injured
item.description: An explosion at a Tyson Foods plant in Georgia late Thursday night left one person dead and others injured.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: More than a third of Americans racked up over $1,000 in debt this holiday season: report
item.description: (KRON) -- More than one third of Americans racked up over $1,000 in credit card debt this holiday season, according to a new report. The report from LendingTree indicates that 36% of Americans took on an average of $1,181 in debt, up from $1,028 in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Bird flu virus in Louisiana patient likely mutated to be more transmissible, CDC says
item.description: The agency, in its latest update released Thursday, said the mutations were not found in samples taken from wild birds, suggesting the virus changed over the course of the patient’s infection.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KRON4

item.title: First severe human bird flu case shows mutations: CDC
item.description: Health experts are now warning pet owners to be cautious about raw foods and limit pets' exposure to wildlife.
item.dc:creator: Nancy Loo, NewsNation

item.title: Massachusetts family found dead from carbon monoxide exposure
item.description: A family from Newton, Massachusetts, have been identified as the victims found dead inside a home on Christmas Day after an apparent exposure to carbon monoxide.
item.dc:creator: Melanie DaSilva, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Kay Granger revelation punctuates increasing scrutiny of aging officials
item.description: The revelation that Rep. Kay Granger (R-Texas) has been living in an assisted living facility is fueling scrutiny of aging public officials, highlighting the shifting norms surrounding lawmaker health, seniority, and for how long it is appropriate to hang on to power.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Pennsylvania teacher jailed in Russia formally designated as wrongfully detained
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An American schoolteacher arrested in Russia on drug charges more than four years ago has been designated by the U.S. government as wrongfully detained, the State Department said Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Onion-wielding man disrupts Christmas church services, pours whiskey into holy water: deputies
item.description: A Maryland man was arrested and charged with several offences stemming from disruptions at two Catholic churches on Christmas Eve, including one disruption which he is accused of pouring liquor into a container of holy water.
item.dc:creator: Alaina Papazian, KRON4

item.title: Fox News hosts Sean Hannity, Ainsley Earhardt announce engagement
item.description: "We are overjoyed and so thankful to our families for all of their love and support during this wonderful time in our lives," the couple said.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: US homelessness up 18% as affordable housing remains out of reach for many people
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — The United States saw an 18.1% increase in homelessness this year, a dramatic rise driven mostly by a lack of affordable housing as well as devastating natural disasters and a surge of migrants in several parts of the country, federal officials said Friday. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Man indicted in burning death of woman inside New York City subway train: prosecutors
item.description: The indictment comes days after Sebastian Zapeta’s arrest and subsequent police questioning, in which authorities say he claimed not to know what had happened but identified himself in photos and surveillance video showing the fire being lit.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Retailers, chains that announced closures in 2024
item.description: It was another tough year for restaurants and traditional brick-and-mortar retailers. Several national chains announced closures and bankruptcies in 2024 – not to mention the local small businesses that quietly shuttered around the country.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, Fox 5

item.title: Azerbaijani airliner with 67 people onboard crashes in Kazakhstan leaving 32 survivors
item.description: An Azerbaijani airliner with 67 people onboard crashed Wednesday near the Kazakhstani city of Aktau, leaving at least 32 survivors, according to officials. More than 30 people may be dead.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
